extra.reasoning:
The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds across the entire reachable state space.

Analysis of the acc update functions: each step transition reduces (3*acc + c) modulo 16 via three nested conditional subtractions of 16 (which fully normalizes any value < 64 into [0,16)). So step_0 = (3*acc+2) mod 16, step_1 = (3*acc+10) mod 16, step_2 = (3*acc+6) mod 16. The additive constants 2, 10, 6 are all even, and acc starts at 0 (even). Since 3*even + even = even, and even mod 16 stays even, acc is invariantly even. The reachable acc values are exactly {0,2,4,6,8,10,12,14}.

sent can be set to true by only two transitions: t2 (send_attempt, requires NOT opted_out) and t4 (send_attempt, requires opted_out AND acc==13). t4 is the only transition that sets sent=true while opted_out is true. But acc==13 is odd and therefore unreachable — t4 is dead code and never fires. Every other transition that touches sent (t1 optout, t3 send_attempt-while-opted-out, t5/t6/t7 steps) sets sent=false.

Exhaustive BFS over the full state machine (booleans opted_out, sent and acc in [0,15]) yields 24 reachable states. In all 8 reachable states with opted_out=true, sent=false. No state with (opted_out AND sent) is reachable at any depth, so the bounded check (18 events) and the unbounded check both pass.
